question: A single onboarding microservice contains a REST endpoint that starts a process, the BPMN definition itself, and glue code for two service tasks. What does the docs say about the job worker for those service tasks?

options.text: It is started automatically as part of the application to handle the subscriptions.
It must be deployed as a separate microservice and connected to the main app over gRPC.
It only starts after the first process instance is created at runtime.
It runs inside the Zeebe broker itself and does not need the application to be running.

a.text: The docs say a job worker is started automatically as part of the application to handle the subscriptions in this typical onboarding-style microservice layout.

b.text: The doc shows the worker bundled inside the same microservice, not as a separate gRPC-connected service.

c.text: Workers open subscriptions at start-up; they are not lazily created on first instance.

d.text: Workers live in the application, not in the broker — Zeebe is described as a remote system to the application.

explanation: When you bundle the REST endpoint, the process definition (typically auto-deployed at start-up), and the service-task glue code in one application, the docs say the job worker is started automatically as part of that application to handle the subscriptions for those service tasks.

docs.title: Connecting the workflow engine with your world
